subject: Re: FTC phone switch upgrade
body:
do it

----- Original Message ----
From: Lesley Groff <lesley@nysgllc.com>
To: J. Epstein <jeeproject@yahoo.com>
Sent: Tuesday, February 6, 2007 8:48:09 AM
Subject: FW: FTC phone switch upgrade

Lesley Groff

Epstein

 

 




From: David Lampert 
Sent: Monday, 
February 05, 2007 4:46 PM
To: Lesley Groff
Subject: FTC 
phone switch upgrade






Les, please forward to JE.  
Thanks.
 
  
 
  
 
  
 
  
 
Rough cost: 25-30k depending on how 
much I have them do at LSJ, too.
 
T1 monthly is 1200k w 50 direct dial 
numbers (that’s the minimum) –with Innovative
 
I will be dangling the T1 over their 
head until LSJ is completed (fiber project), then we’ll jump right into 
this.  I’ll be planning in the interim with the 
contractors.
 
  
 
Problems
 
The only person on island to support 
it is Steve Scully
 
Difficulty finding replacement parts 
and pieces
 
Suffers from shorts and dropped 
calls currently, phone quality issues
 
Requests from staff to add features 
can not be completed as phones features are maxed 
out
 
Anna is the only individual with the 
ability to manage the system as an operator, Daphne and one other person want to 
be able to handle calls  i.e. like Helen, Lesley, and 
Rhana
 
Can’t transfer calls, need to put 
them on hold, page, and have the recipient pick up?
 
  
 
New 
features
 
New switch –we are starting to 
upgrade phones company wide with same platform
 
We can take advantage of the IP 
functionality
 
Voice 
mail
 
Desktop 
faxing
 
Email integration with directory and 
messaging
 
Engineers doing setup could also 
assist in setting up additional LSJ features i.e. IP phones, desktop faxing, 
etc.
 
Additional features for 
phones
 
Full phone feature functionality ie. 
Transfer, speed dials, etc. (many features are self 
serviceable)
 
Utilization of T1 gives everyone 
direct dial capability (ability to receive direct calls at their own 
extension)
 
Utilization of T1 improves call 
quality –as does new phone switch and phones
 
Better phones … they suffer from the 
regurgitation of old, refurbed phones 
 
  
 
Bottom line, as we upgrade our 
switches, Jermaine and I certify, and we grow our networking services … these 
devices are ready to integrate with more features and function.  We are 
making progress on cutting edge communications.  There are more and more 
things that can be added: ability to go from cell to desktop phone seamlessly, 
instant messaging, maintaining the same features and extensions at all of our 
locations (like logging in), remotely maintaining extensions –Lesley at 
home.  My new approach to this stuff and your requests is pretty much: dump 
it on.  As I make my rounds I try to whittle away at improving each 
function.
 
  







 
____________________________________________________________________________________

body_html:
do it

----- Original Message ----
From: Lesley Groff <lesley@nysgllc.com>
To: J. Epstein <jeeproject@yahoo.com>
Sent: Tuesday, February 6, 2007 8:48:09 AM
Subject: FW: FTC phone switch upgrade

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Lesley Groff
Executive Assistant to Jeffrey Epstein
 
 

From: David Lampert
Sent: Monday, February 05, 2007 4:46 PM
To: Lesley Groff
Subject: FTC phone switch upgrade

Les, please forward to JE.  Thanks.

 

 

 

 

Rough cost: 25-30k depending on how much I have them do at LSJ, too.

T1 monthly is 1200k w 50 direct dial numbers (that’s the minimum) –with Innovative

I will be dangling the T1 over their head until LSJ is completed (fiber project), then we’ll jump right into this.  I’ll be planning in the interim with the contractors.

 

Problems

The only person on island to support it is Steve Scully

Difficulty finding replacement parts and pieces

Suffers from shorts and dropped calls currently, phone quality issues

Requests from staff to add features can not be completed as phones features are maxed out

Anna is the only individual with the ability to manage the system as an operator, Daphne and one other person want to be able to handle calls  i.e. like Helen, Lesley, and Rhana

Can’t transfer calls, need to put them on hold, page, and have the recipient pick up?

 

New features

New switch –we are starting to upgrade phones company wide with same platform

We can take advantage of the IP functionality

Voice mail

Desktop faxing

Email integration with directory and messaging

Engineers doing setup could also assist in setting up additional LSJ features i.e. IP phones, desktop faxing, etc.

Additional features for phones

Full phone feature functionality ie. Transfer, speed dials, etc. (many features are self serviceable)

Utilization of T1 gives everyone direct dial capability (ability to receive direct calls at their own extension)

Utilization of T1 improves call quality –as does new phone switch and phones

Better phones … they suffer from the regurgitation of old, refurbed phones

 

Bottom line, as we upgrade our switches, Jermaine and I certify, and we grow our networking services … these devices are ready to integrate with more features and function.  We are making progress on cutting edge communications.  There are more and more things that can be added: ability to go from cell to desktop phone seamlessly, instant messaging, maintaining the same features and extensions at all of our locations (like logging in), remotely maintaining extensions –Lesley at home.  My new approach to this stuff and your requests is pretty much: dump it on.  As I make my rounds I try to whittle away at improving each function.

 



8:00? 8:25? 8:40? Find a flick in no time
with theYahoo! Search movie showtime shortcut.
